figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of the Ω framework. The EnKF-CGNS forecast produces an augmented ensemble {V(i) f,k} = {(X(i) f,k,µ (i) f,k)}. A residual score operator sθ trained through Noise2Noise denoising score matching corrects the augmented state by annealed Langevin MCMC. The corrected conditional means {µ(i) a,k} then parametrize Gaussian conditionals from which latent samples are drawn analytically, producing a non-Gaussian Gaussian-mixture posterior for Yk.

Table 1. Information propagation pathways in the Ω framework at assimilation time tk. Vk = (Xk,µk) is the augmented state; R(i) f,k is CGNS forecast covariance for latent variable; Pf,k is the empirical augmented forecast covariance (3.10); Robs is the observation noise covariance; Ck = (V̄f,k,diag(Pf,k),Zk) is the context vector (4.8). Notation from Sections 3–4.

Fig. 2. Closed-loop assimilation of the observed variable x for the dyad model. Shown are the true trajectory (black), the Ω posterior mean (red), the EnKF posterior mean (blue), and the particle filter posterior mean (green).

Fig. 3. Reconstruction of the hidden variable y for the dyad model. The Ω posterior mean (red) and particle filter mean (green) both closely track the latent trajectory. Shading shows ±2 posterior standard deviations for Ω.

Fig. 4. Marginal probability density functions of x (a) and y (b) accumulated over all assimilation times. The Gaussian-mixture posterior generated by Ω reproduces the asymmetric and heavy-tailed structure of the particle-filter reference distribution, whereas the EnKF shows larger errors in estimating the probability of extreme events.
Fig. 5. Conditional posterior distributions of the hidden variable y at three selected assimilation times.

Fig. 6. Posterior mean trajectories of representative hidden spectral modes. Ω more accurately reconstructs intermittent fluctuations and extreme excursions than the EnKF, particularly during periods of enhanced nonlinear activity. The EnKF uncertainty band is omitted for visual clarity and overall easier interpretation.

Fig. 7. Snapshot of the reconstructed streamfunction field ψ(x, y, t) at a representative time instance. The field is obtained from the Fourier-mode representation of the model and illustrates the spatial organization of the large-scale circulation and wave structures.

Fig. 8. Conditional distribution of a representative hidden vorticity mode. The asymmetric heavy-tailed structure associated with intermittent dynamics is accurately captured by Ω but is significantly underestimated by the EnKF.
Table 3. RMSE for representative hidden variables of the stochastic Lorenz–96 system.

Fig. 9. Posterior mean trajectories for representative observed and hidden variables of the stochastic Lorenz 96 system. The residual correction in Ω substantially improves the reconstruction of hidden variables relative to the surrogate-only CGNSEnKF and is slosely comparble with the EnKF applied on the on the perfect model. The behavior is the same across all other variables Xi, Yi, i = 1, · · · , 10.
Fig. 10. Hovmőller diagram of mean trajectories of the stochastic Lorenz 96 system. The Ω results are very close to the truth and the EnKF applied on the perfect model.
